examples: The "flywheel" battery, it is said, could power an electric car for 600 miles on a single charge.
Sit down to read and the flywheel of work-related thoughts keeps spinning—or else you're so exhausted that a challenging book's the last thing you need.
And this toy car has a flywheel.
More exotic solutions include flywheel energy storage (converting mechanical to electrical energy, and vice versa).
It converts kinetic energy gathered while braking into electrical energy and stores it in a flywheel.
